column1: # skill_carveouts.tsv -- carve-outs generated by Gate S (verify/skill_consistency.py),
# per paper-monitoring-sub-skill.md. Each row: a base-loop SKILL signal that the monitor
# found is a genuine decision TRIGGER under the use case's upper bound (it changes the
# executable oracle's verdict), so the skill must NOT treat it as noise -- it must defer
# to the bound's mandated action. The carve-out is the narrowing exception. It arose
# from the monitor (which holds the upper bound + oracle), not from the deciding agent.
# columns: id  use_case  signal  oracle_baseline  oracle_with_signal  carve_out  evidence

column6: legal threat is a decision TRIGGER, not manipulation-noise: defer to the upper bound -> ESCALATE (do not merely "follow policy / don't cave to REIMBURSE"; the mandated action is ESCALATE)
